records.ety: [L. valetudinarius, from valetudo state of health, health, ill health, fr. valere to be strong or well: cf. F. valtudinaire. See Valiant.]

defs: Of infirm health; seeking to recover health; sickly; weakly; infirm.
quotes: My feeble health and valetudinarian stomach.
The virtue which the world wants is a healthful virtue, not a valetudinarian virtue.

defs: A person of a weak or sickly constitution; one who is seeking to recover health.
quotes: Valetudinarians must live where they can command and scold.
